title: CAWS Inter-Agent Message
description: One line in .caws/messages.jsonl — a directed message between two CAWS sessions (or a session and a named human/automation client). This log is SEPARATE from events.jsonl by design: chat-rate traffic must never dilute the integrity-critical audit chain. Two record kinds share the file: a 'message' record (a directed send) and a 'delivery' record (marks a message consumed). Like events, the actor is a structured, kernel-attributed envelope so a message's PROVENANCE (who sent it) is recorded rather than self-claimed — but the channel does NOT vouch for the message BODY, which a consumer must treat as an unverified claim.

record.description: Discriminator. 'message': a directed send (carries id/actor/to/text/channel). 'delivery': marks a prior message id consumed by its recipient.

ts.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp. Informational ordering aid; the file's line order is authoritative for replay.

id.description: Unique message id (UUID). Required on 'message' records; absent on 'delivery' records (which reference via deliver_id).

kind.description: Sender class, mirroring the events envelope actor.

session_id.description: Sender session id (e.g. CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID). This is the sender's address on the channel.

actor.description: Sender's structured actor. Required on 'message' records. Records WHO sent the message (provenance), not whether the body is true.

to.description: Recipient endpoint id (a session_id, or a named human/automation client). Required on 'message' records.

channel.description: Normalized unordered pair of the two endpoint ids joined by '::' (sorted), so A->B and B->A share one channel. Required on 'message' records. Derived, not user-supplied.

text.description: Opaque message body. The channel attributes the sender but does not vouch for this content. Required on 'message' records.

deliver_id.description: On 'delivery' records: the id of the message that was consumed. Required on 'delivery' records.
